sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of chronic lumbar back pain for approximately two years, initially following furniture moving. He describes the pain as sharp and stabbing, radiating down his right leg to his toe. He experiences numbness and tingling in his right leg and foot. He denies any weakness or bowel/bladder dysfunction. He reports a family history of spine conditions.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals midline tenderness at L4-L5 with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness. Straight leg raise test is positive on the right. Neurological exam shows decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome. Knee and ankle reflexes are symmetrical. MRI reveals disk herniation with nerve impingement.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disc with radiculopathy, causing nerve impingement.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am recommending a spinal injection with a corticosteroid under fluoroscopy to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. We will monitor the patient's response to the injection and limit injections to no more than four times per year. I have prescribed a strong inflammatory medication as a corticosteroid for injection.
